# Postman/Newman Sandbox Corpus

These fixtures are exported Postman Collection v2.1 JSON documents used as the sandbox v1 compatibility corpus. The current audited Desktop reference target is Postman Desktop 11.71.7 with `postman-sandbox@6.2.2` and Postman Runtime 7.50.0. The upstream Newman reference target is the current npm `latest` dist-tag resolved by the live docs audit: `newman@6.2.2` with Postman Runtime 7.39.1.

- `newman-sandbox-v1.collection.json` covers async ordering, timers/microtasks, nested `pm.sendRequest`, iteration data, current-URL and jar-style cookie helpers including hostname and object-set forms, variable precedence, variable metadata/clear/toJSON behavior, documented dynamic variables, Postman globals, version-pinned bundled `pm.require`/`require` packages, Postman-listed NodeJS module facades, Postman Collection SDK request/response/list behavior, reviewed cached team/external package imports, reviewed multi-file CommonJS package imports, Chai-compatible assertions, `pm.test.skip`/`pm.test.index`/skipped result shape, `pm.execution.location`, `pm.execution.runRequest`, Handlebars visualizer compile/precompile/template/helper/partial/decorator/inline-script/reviewed-asset behavior, brokered `pm.vault`, execution control, skipped requests, and mixed `pm.test` pass/fail behavior.
- `newman-sandbox-v1.iteration-data.json` is the data row used by the main corpus run.
- `newman-sandbox-v1.expected.json` records the stable PostMeter/Newman-style run outcomes asserted by automated tests.
- `newman-cancellation.collection.json` covers cancellation of pending async sandbox work.
- `differential-http-core.collection.json` is the small HTTP-core differential fixture used by `npm run postman:parity:diff`. It covers request header mutation, response assertions, `pm.info`, environment mutation, collection-variable access, and `pm.sendRequest` callback behavior against a local brokered HTTP server.
- `differential-sandbox-broad.collection.json` is the broader Newman-compatible differential fixture used by `npm run postman:parity:diff`. It covers package loading, assertion helpers, timers, dynamic variables, cookie jar helpers, request mutation, `pm.sendRequest` object bodies, and caught failure paths.
- `differential-dynamic-host-globals.collection.json` is the Newman-compatible dynamic-code and host-like globals differential fixture. It covers direct eval, indirect eval, `Function`, lexical `Buffer`, raw network/browser globals, `process`, `globalThis`, WebAssembly, and constructor-escape behavior.
- `differential-runtime-limits.collection.json` is the Newman-compatible runtime-limits differential fixture. It covers async timer drain, cleared intervals, console method availability, and visualizer output below PostMeter's explicit security budgets.
- `differential-httponly-cookies.collection.json` is the Newman-compatible `HttpOnly` cookie differential fixture. It covers script readability through `pm.cookies` and `pm.cookies.jar()`, CookieList `getAll`, jar replacement/unset/clear, normal imported-request jar sends, and `pm.sendRequest` response-cookie jar updates.
- `differential-sendrequest-advanced.collection.json` is the Newman-compatible advanced `pm.sendRequest` fixture. It covers Digest challenge retry, Hawk, and AWS Signature v4 helpers through script-created brokered requests; OAuth 1.0 has focused PostMeter sender coverage because `newman@6.2.2` did not apply that helper in the live differential probe.
- `differential-sendrequest-files.collection.json` is the Newman-compatible file-binding fixture. It covers imported binary/file-style bodies and multipart form-data file parts through user-granted parent-side bindings; focused PostMeter sender tests cover brokered `pm.sendRequest` file bindings because `newman@6.2.2` reports script-originated file uploads as disallowed.
- `protocol-script-hooks.collection.json` covers imported GraphQL Before query/After response scripts and gRPC Before invoke/On message/After response scripts, including package imports, protocol request mutation, metadata/messages, response metadata/trailers/messages, `pm.message`, and protocol-specific `pm.info.eventName` values. Live parent-owned gRPC transport coverage for unary/client-streaming/server-streaming/bidirectional calls lives in `test/electron/grpcClient.test.js`, with the desktop evidence artifact recorded under `desktop-observations/postman-desktop-11.71.7-grpc-transport-audit.json`.
- `local-mock-scripts.collection.json` covers imported local mock scripts, method/path-only matching, path variables, saved example responses, top-level mock-script `await`/`return`, `pm.mock.matchRequest`, `pm.mock.sendExample`, and persistent transaction-safe `pm.state` behavior.
- `real-world-import-corpus.collection.json` is a broad representative import fixture for production-claim verification. It combines auth-heavy scripts, reviewed package references, dynamic variables, cookie metadata, visualizer assets, vault metadata, GraphQL hooks, gRPC hooks, local mock scripts, `runRequest` workflows, certificates, and file/binary body references without importing third-party copyrighted collection content.
- `docs/postman-docs-coverage-audit.json` is the generated official-docs token inventory used with the fixture/matrix evidence. Regenerate it with `npm run postman:docs:write`, validate the committed mapping with `npm run postman:docs:validate`, and run `npm run postman:docs:live` before repeating a current upstream Postman/Newman parity claim.
- `desktop-observations/` contains the template path for APIs that Newman does not support and that need current Postman Desktop observations before parity rows can be marked complete. Rows that point only to `observation-template.json` are not complete evidence for the 1:1 claim. `desktop-observations/postman-desktop-11.71.7-runtime-source-audit.json` records the installed Postman Desktop runtime/source audit used by the matrix validator for implemented desktop-required rows. `desktop-observations/postman-desktop-11.71.7-dynamic-host-globals-audit.json` records the Desktop/Newman dynamic-code and host-like globals audit. `desktop-observations/postman-desktop-11.71.7-sendrequest-advanced-audit.json` records the advanced `pm.sendRequest` auth/proxy/TLS/client-certificate classification. `desktop-observations/postman-desktop-11.71.7-sendrequest-files-audit.json` records the file/binary/form-data binding policy and evidence. `desktop-observations/postman-desktop-11.71.7-visualizer-handlebars-audit.json` records visualizer Handlebars parity evidence. `desktop-observations/postman-desktop-11.71.7-commonjs-package-audit.json` records reviewed multi-file CommonJS package-loader semantics and evidence. `desktop-observations/websocket-script-support-audit.json` records the current official-docs finding that WebSocket and Socket.IO requests do not expose saved/importable script hooks.

When maintainers provide canonical Newman JSON exports for the broader corpus fixtures, generate them with `newman@6.2.2`, add them beside the current expected file, and keep the automated assertions aligned with the implemented safe sandbox subset. The differential harness can also generate live Newman output with `npm run postman:parity:diff -- --newman --download-newman`. `pm.vault` is treated as a Postman sandbox parity fixture because current Newman/Postman CLI runs do not expose vault access.
